refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these obligations are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation of performance requirements, which is a distinct functional need independent of how the requirements are recorded or managed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze worksite mission context inputs and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability without specifying how these are stored or managed.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the history of performance requirements including baselines and calibration updates.

candidate_dps: Define a performance requirements derivation process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets
Establish a traceable documentation framework linking worksite inputs to performance requirements
Implement a baseline and calibration update management mechanism to maintain history
Integrate safety plan and operational policy constraints into performance requirement derivation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a performance requirements derivation process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between mission context inputs and safety policies
Dependency on calibration update history for accurate performance targets
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates the derivation process from documentation and update management, supporting modularity. Safety and policy constraints are inputs to the derivation process, maintaining independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission context inputs are well-defined and accessible
Risk that changes in safety plans or policies may require frequent updates to derivation process
Assumes calibration update history is accurately maintained and integrated
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating documentation and update management as distinct DPs to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic and processing, including the derivation of performance requirements from mission inputs.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission input data and worksite status for performance target derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends derived performance targets and task assignments to other modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the performance requirements derivation is a core system function; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module is focused on physical control and execution, not on performance requirements derivation; would cause high diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and operator interface, not on performance requirements derivation; poor containment.
Safety Services is specialized for safety monitoring and alerts, unrelated to performance requirements derivation; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the performance requirements derivation process because it centralizes system-level logic and minimizes diffusion across other specialized modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Tasking interface responsibilities if task assignment logic is not clearly separated.
If performance requirements derivation requires real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation, interface complexity may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly defines a process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets, directly addressing the FR requirement to derive performance requirements from the mission context. The mapping is logically sound and traceable.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Tasking interface responsibilities if task assignment logic is not clearly separated.
If performance requirements derivation requires real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation, interface complexity may increase.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1) for the performance requirements derivation process, indicating good architectural packaging. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives mission input data via Telemetry and outputs derived performance targets via Tasking, enabling traceability and documentation of performance targets as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the performance requirements derivation function to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well justified, with minimal diffusion and strong cohesion.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the separation of responsibilities between performance requirements derivation and task assignment within the NGHE Solution module to mitigate overlap risks.
Define clear interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to manage real-time data dependencies and reduce interface complexity.

Include explicit traceability mechanisms within the NGHE Solution module to link derived performance targets to worksite inputs and maintain history of baselines/calibration updates as per acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with clear design parameters and implementation modules. The NGHE Solution module demonstrates high containment and low diffusion, ensuring good architectural packaging. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the identified risks are manageable through recommended revisions rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and traceable design at this level is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: To clarify and document separation of responsibilities within the NGHE Solution module, define clear interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, and include explicit traceability mechanisms to mitigate identified risks and support verification.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the performance requirements derivation function effectively.
Interface complexity can be managed through clear contracts without further decomposition.
Verification processes are sufficient at this level to ensure safety-critical requirements are met.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Tasking interface responsibilities if separation is not clearly documented.
Interface complexity due to real-time data dependencies with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Documentation of responsibility separation within NGHE Solution module.
Defined interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Traceability mechanisms linking derived performance targets to worksite inputs and baseline updates.
